checkpoint_arrival.label: CHECKPOINT
checkpoint_arrival.text: Smoke stitches the morning sky above the checkpoint. Your unit is scattered, command is static, and civilians press against the barricade with eyes that have already seen too much. Someone pounds on the gate. Someone behind you whispers that the dead are learning to run.

choices.text: Hold the gate and organize the civilians
Raid the armory before command collapses
Slip out through the drainage ditch

suburb_arrival.label: SUBURB
suburb_arrival.text: The suburbs look almost normal from a distance. Curtains move. Dogs bark from locked houses. Then you notice the blood on the welcome mats and the grocery cart overturned in the middle of the road. Home is a word with teeth now.

choices.text: Search houses quietly for supplies
Follow the crying from the blue house
Get to the main road before dark

overpass_arrival.label: UNDERPASS
overpass_arrival.text: Rain drums on the concrete ribs of the overpass. The people who slept here last night left in a hurry: blankets, shoes, a dented pot still warm over a dying trash fire. On the wall, someone wrote WE SHARE OR WE STARVE.

choices.text: Take what was left behind
Leave a warning sign and move on
Wait under the bridge for the owners

school_lockdown.label: LOCKDOWN
school_lockdown.text: The intercom clicks every few minutes, but no voice follows. Desks are wedged against the door. Down the hall, lockers rattle like someone is dragging a chain across them. A message in marker on the window reads: THEY ARE IN THE GYM.

choices.text: Lead everyone through the library windows
Use the PA system to draw infected away
Barricade in place and ration snacks

mall_arrival.label: MALL
mall_arrival.text: The food court is a mural of spilled soda and abandoned shoes. Security gates hang half-closed. From the sporting goods store comes the steady beep of a register, still asking for payment while the world forgets what money meant.

choices.text: Arm yourself in sporting goods
Look for survivors in the food court
Take the loading corridor outside

hospital_shift.label: HOSPITAL
hospital_shift.text: The hospital generator coughs. Emergency lights paint every face red. Triage tags run out before the bodies do. Behind the double doors, a patient flatlines, then sits up with a sound no living throat should make.

choices.text: Evacuate the children from pediatrics
Lock the ward and take medical supplies
Use the ambulance bay to escape

ambulance_bay.label: AMBULANCE BAY
ambulance_bay.text: Ambulances sit nose-out like horses waiting for a starting gun. One has keys in the visor. Another has someone trapped inside, alive enough to bang weakly on the glass.

choices.text: Free the trapped paramedic
Take the working ambulance and go
Strip the ambulances for supplies

clinic_lockin.label: CLINIC
clinic_lockin.text: Your clinic has one exam room, two exits, and a waiting area full of people who still believe professionals know what to do. The radio says stay calm. The street outside says run.

choices.text: Treat whoever you can before leaving
Convert the clinic into a hideout
Empty the pharmacy cabinet

clinic_hideout.label: CLINIC HIDEOUT
clinic_hideout.text: By midnight the clinic has rules taped to the door: knock twice, no fever, no lies. It is small, but it has clean counters, locked cabinets, and enough chairs for people to pretend waiting still means help is coming.

choices.text: Open the clinic to wounded strangers
Keep it hidden as your private fallback
Trade treatment for food and protection

station_riot.label: STATION
station_riot.text: The holding cells are full of bitten people and people accused of being bitten. The difference matters less every minute. A rookie is crying by the evidence locker, asking who gets saved first.

choices.text: Release the uninfected prisoners
Secure the evidence locker
Escort the rookie out

roadblock_arrival.label: ROADBLOCK
roadblock_arrival.text: Cars are packed bumper to bumper beneath dead traffic lights. Someone tried to make a roadblock out of school buses. Someone else tried to drive through it. Neither plan worked.

choices.text: Search trunks before nightfall
Clear a path for anyone still alive
Climb the buses and scout ahead

garage_start.label: GARAGE
garage_start.text: The garage smells like gasoline, oil, and panic. A family pounds on the roll-up door while a dead customer crawls beneath the lift. There is an old delivery van in bay three. It might run if you make it believe.

choices.text: Repair the delivery van
Let the family inside
Grab tools and flee through the office

tow_yard.label: TOW YARD
tow_yard.text: Rows of impounded cars glitter under security lights. The fence keeps infected out for now. It also keeps you in with a man in the office who has a shotgun and a very small definition of community.

choices.text: Offer repairs in exchange for shelter
Steal fuel from the lot
Cut through the fence and leave

bus_depot.label: BUS DEPOT
bus_depot.text: The depot loudspeaker loops a cancellation notice. Inside, a city bus waits with a full tank and a cracked windshield. The route sign still reads DOWNTOWN, which feels like a dare.

choices.text: Start the bus and make a rescue route
Strip the depot for parts
Use the roof as a quiet camp

museum_start.label: MUSEUM
museum_start.text: A school field trip becomes a siege among dinosaur bones and glass cases. Children hide beneath the fossil whale. The security guard offers you a ring of keys with shaking hands.

choices.text: Move the children through the staff tunnel
Barricade the exhibit hall
Take the keys and scout alone

school_empty.label: AFTER HOURS
school_empty.text: The school after hours was always eerie. Now the trophy case is broken, the nurse's office is open, and one classroom has a message written in chalk: DO NOT TRUST THE BUS DRIVER.

choices.text: Raid the nurse's office
Check the buses anyway
Leave a map for whoever returns

stadium_practice.label: STADIUM
stadium_practice.text: Practice ends when the first scream rolls across the bleachers. The coach locks the equipment room. Fans flood the field. The tunnel to the street is open, but so is the gate to the shelter under the stands.

choices.text: Sprint for the street
Break open the equipment room
Guide the crowd under the stands

gym_start.label: GYM
gym_start.text: The gym doors are chained from the outside. The locker room has protein bars, towels, and one narrow window painted shut. Outside, shoes squeak across the court with no rhythm at all.

choices.text: Force the window open
Make padding from towels and tape
Wait until the noise passes

job_center.label: JOB CENTER
job_center.text: The job center is full of laminated optimism: BUILD YOUR FUTURE, START TODAY. The future starts with vending machine crackers and a locked emergency exit.

choices.text: Crack the vending machines
Help strangers climb the emergency exit
Read the notice board for leads

restaurant_start.label: KITCHEN
restaurant_start.text: The line cooks left burners on and prayers half-finished. The dining room windows are smeared with handprints. In the walk-in freezer, someone is begging to be let out. They will not say if they were bitten.

choices.text: Open the freezer and check them
Pack food before the power dies
Use the back alley exit

food_truck.label: FOOD TRUCK
food_truck.text: The food truck is parked sideways across an intersection, its grill cold and its propane tank half full. A child points at it from a second-floor window and mouths one word: hungry.

choices.text: Cook what you can and share it
Take the propane for later
Trade food for information

market_start.label: MARKET
market_start.text: The produce market rots in beautiful colors. Flies rise like ash when you move. A vendor with a bandaged arm guards the canned goods and asks what your conscience is worth.

choices.text: Trade fairly with the vendor
Distract the vendor and steal cans
Treat the bandaged arm first

server_room.label: SERVER ROOM
server_room.text: The servers keep humming after everyone stops answering email. A municipal alert terminal still has power. You can send one message before the backup batteries die.

choices.text: Broadcast shelter coordinates
Download city utility maps
Use batteries for your own kit

bridge_start.label: BRIDGE
bridge_start.text: The north bridge groans under abandoned cars and desperate footsteps. The army wired charges along the supports before they vanished. A crowd is still crossing.

choices.text: Defuse enough charges to keep it standing
Warn the crowd and run
Mark the weak supports on your map

power_plant.label: POWER PLANT
power_plant.text: The power plant fence hums with one last dirty miracle. Inside, lights still burn over empty desks. Outside, a neighborhood watches the skyline, hoping someone keeps the cold away.

choices.text: Stabilize the substation for the neighborhood
Take parts for a portable generator
Leave before the plant draws infected

barricade_hold.label: THE GATE
barricade_hold.text: You hold the line until holding becomes choosing. A bitten father asks you to let his daughter through. A soldier raises a rifle. The daughter is not bitten. The father knows he is.

choices.text: Let the girl in and keep the father outside
Let them both in and isolate him
Close the gate on everyone

armory_choice.label: ARMORY
armory_choice.text: The armory is a steel room full of terrible answers. Weapons will keep you alive. Weapons will also make every hungry person look at you differently.

choices.text: Take only what you can hide
Arm a small civilian guard
Keep the best weapons for yourself

quiet_scavenge.label: OPEN DRAWERS
quiet_scavenge.text: You learn the new language of empty homes: aspirin in the bathroom, batteries in junk drawers, grief in refrigerator photos. In one kitchen, a handwritten note says TAKE WHAT YOU NEED, LEAVE WHAT YOU CAN.

choices.text: Leave supplies for the next survivor
Take everything useful
Spend time looking for family records

blue_house.label: BLUE HOUSE
blue_house.text: The crying belongs to an elderly woman trapped behind a collapsed bookshelf. Her son is infected in the hallway, still wearing a birthday hat. She asks you not to hurt him where she can see.

choices.text: Help her out and respect her request
Put the son down quickly
Leave before the hallway fills

library_escape.label: LIBRARY
library_escape.text: Books rain from shelves as people squeeze through the windows. Someone drops a backpack. Someone else reaches back for it. The infected are close enough that every small mercy has a price tag.

choices.text: Go back for the backpack
Keep everyone moving
Split off alone through the alley

noise_lure.label: PA SYSTEM
noise_lure.text: Your voice booms through the school, cracking on the final word. The infected turn as one. For ten seconds, the halls belong to the living. Then the speakers shriek and die.

choices.text: Run with the crowd
Stay behind to hold the door
Hide in the nurse's office

classroom_night.label: ROOM 214
classroom_night.text: Night puts every sound under a magnifying glass. A student offers you their last candy bar. Another asks whether their parents are dead. You have no answer that does not taste like ash.

choices.text: Tell the truth: you do not know
Promise their parents are coming
Eat your share and sleep

mall_sporting.label: SPORTING GOODS
mall_sporting.text: Bats, arrows, camping fuel, rain ponchos: civilization sold apocalypse kits by accident. A clerk is hiding beneath the counter with the safe keys and a bleeding bite on his wrist.

choices.text: Use the keys, then lock him somewhere quiet
Try to treat the bite
Leave the store before he turns

food_court_group.label: FOOD COURT
food_court_group.text: Six survivors have made a fort out of overturned tables. They have children, panic, and not enough food. Their leader asks if you are useful or just hungry.

choices.text: Join their scavenge rotation
Trade supplies and leave
Threaten them for a bigger share

pediatric_ward.label: PEDIATRICS
pediatric_ward.text: The pediatric ward smells like disinfectant and crayons. You carry one child. Two more hold your coat. A parent blocks the stairwell, bitten and begging you to save them too.

choices.text: Risk helping the parent down the stairs
Save the children first
Hand the children to staff and leave

pharmacy_run.label: PHARMACY
pharmacy_run.text: The pharmacy shelves are a cruel kind of treasure. Insulin, antibiotics, sedatives, painkillers. You cannot carry enough for everyone. Maybe no one can.

choices.text: Take broad-use medicine for trade
Leave insulin labeled for patients
Set up a tiny treatment station

paramedic_saved.label: PARAMEDIC
paramedic_saved.text: The paramedic gives you a route map marked with blocked streets and whispered shelters. They also warn you: the stadium broadcast is real, but so are the raiders listening to it.

choices.text: Head for the stadium broadcast
Avoid crowds and cut through alleys
Stay and help them treat the wounded

evidence_locker.label: EVIDENCE
evidence_locker.text: Inside the locker: cash, weapons, confiscated medicine, and a notebook naming corrupt cops and local gangs. In the old world it was evidence. In this one it is leverage.

choices.text: Use the notebook to negotiate safe passage
Burn the notebook and take medicine
Keep everything for yourself

trunk_search.label: TRUNKS
trunk_search.text: Every trunk is a biography. Soccer cleats. Cat food. A wedding dress. A tire iron wrapped in a towel. One car has a baby seat and a cooler that is still cold.

choices.text: Take the cooler and leave a note
Search for survivors instead of loot
Use the tire iron as a weapon

convoy_choice.label: CONVOY
convoy_choice.text: A chain of cars starts moving behind you. For a moment, the road sounds alive again. Then a family in the lead car runs out of fuel and blocks everyone.

choices.text: Share fuel and keep the convoy together
Push their car aside
Send scouts ahead on foot

van_escape.label: VAN
van_escape.text: The delivery van coughs, protests, and finally turns over. Its side still advertises birthday cakes. People cheer when they hear the engine. You can fill it with people or fill it with supplies.

choices.text: Use the van as a rescue vehicle
Load supplies and leave fast
Trade seats for fuel and food

family_in_garage.label: FAMILY
family_in_garage.text: The family tumbles inside: two parents, one teenager, one grandmother with a garden trowel. They know a church basement where survivors are gathering, if you can get them there.

choices.text: Escort them to the church
Ask for payment before helping
Send them with directions and stay

temporary_shelter.label: SHELTER
temporary_shelter.text: For one night, walls hold. People sleep in shifts. Someone makes a joke and everyone laughs too hard because laughter is also a ration.

choices.text: Take first watch
Patch wounds and share food
Rest while others guard

freezer_mercy.label: FREEZER
freezer_mercy.text: The person in the freezer is shaking, pale, and unbitten. They were locked in by coworkers who panicked. They offer you the restaurant's emergency cash box, then laugh when they remember cash is just paper.

choices.text: Bring them with you
Take food and tell them where to go
Use the freezer as a barricade

bridge_saved.label: STANDING
bridge_saved.text: The bridge holds. People cross in stunned silence, touching the rails like a blessing. A radio operator among them says Haven Base needs people who can keep broken things working.

choices.text: Answer the call to Haven Base
Stay and manage the crossing
Leave before gratitude becomes requests

radio_hint.label: STATIC
radio_hint.text: A broadcast fights through the static: Haven Base, old stadium, food, walls, doctors. Another voice breaks over it, laughing softly, repeating the same coordinates. Hope and bait use the same frequency.

choices.text: Head for Haven Base
Circle the stadium and watch first
Ignore the broadcast and stay mobile

community_thread.label: THREADS
community_thread.text: People begin to remember your name. That can save you. That can bury you under favors. A small group asks you to help turn an old mill by the river into a settlement.

choices.text: Commit to building the mill settlement
Keep helping, but stay independent
Trade your reputation for supplies

city_streets.label: STREETS
city_streets.text: The city has become a machine that runs on fear. Sirens die. Windows stare. Every block asks the same question in a different accent: what are you willing to become by sunset?

choices.text: Scavenge the pharmacy district
Follow signs toward Haven Base
Look for a defensible shelter
Search for other survivors

pharmacy_district.label: PHARMACY ROW
pharmacy_district.text: Three pharmacies sit across from each other like rival churches. One is burned. One is locked. One has a line of sick people outside and a teenager with a crowbar deciding who deserves medicine.

choices.text: Help organize a fair ration line
Break into the locked pharmacy
Trade labor for medicine

haven_approach.label: HAVEN
haven_approach.text: The old stadium rises over the road, ringed with buses and sheet metal. A hand-painted sign reads HAVEN BASE. Another sign, smaller and fresher, reads NO WEAPONS WITHOUT WORK.

choices.text: Enter openly and offer your skills
Hide your best gear before entering
Avoid the gates and watch from outside

stadium_watch.label: WATCHING
stadium_watch.text: From the concession roof, you watch Haven Base for a day. They feed people. They also exile people. At dusk, raiders test the west fence and everyone inside looks very young.

choices.text: Warn Haven about the raiders
Use the attack as cover to slip inside
Leave Haven to its own problems

old_mill.label: OLD MILL
old_mill.text: The mill has water, a stone foundation, and people willing to argue about chore charts. It is absurd. It is fragile. It is the closest thing to a future you have seen.

choices.text: Build the settlement and defend its rules
Turn it into a trading post
Leave before they make you responsible

haven_judgement.label: JUDGEMENT
haven_judgement.text: Haven Base puts your name on a clipboard and your past in the air between you. People you helped speak up. People you hurt speak louder. The gate captain asks one question: what will you do when the food runs low?

choices.text: Share risk and ration by need
Protect your own people first
Reject their judgement and keep walking

last_door.label: LAST DOOR
last_door.text: The door bucks against your shoulder. People escape behind you. Your arms shake. You do not know their names. They will know yours.

choices.text: Let go at the last second and run
Hold until everyone is clear

underpass_return.label: RETURN
underpass_return.text: The owners return: three teenagers and a woman with a crowbar. They expected thieves. They find you waiting. It takes them a long time to lower the crowbar.

choices.text: Join them for the night
Trade information and move on
Ask them to travel with you

ending_community.label: ENDING
ending_community.text: Months later, the mill wheel turns again. Children carry water. Watchfires mark the road. The dead still walk outside the walls, but inside, people argue about tomorrow because they believe tomorrow exists.

ending_trader.label: ENDING
ending_trader.text: Your trading post becomes a rumor that saves lives. Medicine for fuel. Batteries for maps. Mercy for mercy, when people can afford it. You survive by making need speak clearly.

ending_haven.label: ENDING
ending_haven.text: Haven Base lets you through. Not because you are clean. No one is. Because enough people decide you are useful, and enough decide you are worth trusting. It is not salvation, but it has walls.

ending_hardline.label: ENDING
ending_hardline.text: You earn a place among the hardliners. Food goes to workers. Weapons go to loyal hands. People live. Some even thank you. At night, you remember the ones outside the math.

ending_wanderer.label: ENDING
ending_wanderer.text: You keep moving. The city thins into roads, roads into fields, fields into rumors. Every sunrise feels stolen. Every night, you write one more line in the notebook: still here.

ending_sacrifice.label: ENDING
ending_sacrifice.text: They get out because you do not. Later, someone paints your name beside the library window. The letters run in the rain, but the story stays.
